question_text:
1242. Web Crawler Multithreaded

Given a URL startUrl and an interface HtmlParser, implement a Multi-threaded web crawler to crawl all links that are under the same hostname as startUrl.

Return all URLs obtained by your web crawler in any order.

Your crawler should:

  • Start from the page: startUrl
  • Call HtmlParser.getUrls(url) to get all URLs from a webpage of a given URL.
  • Do not crawl the same link twice.
  • Explore only the links that are under the same hostname as startUrl.

As shown in the example URL above, the hostname is example.org. For simplicity's sake, you may assume all URLs use http protocol without any port specified. For example, the URLs http://leetcode.com/problems and http://leetcode.com/contest share the same hostname, i.e., leetcode.com, while URLs http://example.org/foo and http://example.com/ have different hostnames, as example.org and example.com are different.

 

Example 1:

Input:
urls = [
  "http://news.yahoo.com",
  "http://news.yahoo.com/news",
  "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/",
  "http://news.google.com",
  "http://news.yahoo.com/us"
]
edges = [[2,0],[2,1],[3,2],[3,1],[0,4]]
startUrl = "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/"
Output: [
  "http://news.yahoo.com",
  "http://news.yahoo.com/news",
  "http://news.yahoo.com/news/topics/",
  "http://news.yahoo.com/us"
]

 

Constraints:

  • 1 <= urls.length <= 1000
  • 1 <= urls[i].length <= 300
  • startUrl is one of the urls.
  • Hostname label must be from 1 to 63 characters long, and the whole hostname (including dots) must be equal to or less than 253 ASCII characters.
  • At most 2 * 104 calls will be made to getUrls.
input_format: First line startUrl. Subsequent lines: pairs of url neighbor_url (representing an edge in the web graph).
output_format: Sorted list of all reachable URLs under same hostname.
constraints: Hostname of each URL has length at most 50.
explanation: Perform BFS/DFS from startUrl. Extract the hostname from startUrl. For each page visited, crawl all neighbor URLs. Only add URLs that share the same hostname. Use a visited set to prevent cycles. In production this uses ThreadPoolExecutor.

boilerplate.python:
import sys
from collections import deque
from urllib.parse import urlparse

def solve(startUrl, edges):
    pass

if __name__ == '__main__':
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    startUrl = data[0].strip()
    edges = data[1:]
    print(solve(startUrl, edges))
boilerplate.java:
import java.util.*;
public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) {} }

test_cases.input:
http://a.com/
http://a.com/ http://a.com/1
http://a.com/1 http://a.com/2
http://a.com/2 http://a.com/3

http://z.com/start
http://z.com/start http://z.com/a
http://z.com/a http://z.com/b
http://z.com/b http://z.com/start

http://site.com/
http://site.com/ http://site.com/p1
http://site.com/ http://site.com/p2
http://site.com/ http://site.com/p3
http://site.com/ http://site.com/p4
http://site.com/ http://site.com/p5
test_cases.expected_output:
http://site.com/
http://site.com/p1
http://site.com/p2
http://site.com/p3
http://site.com/p4
http://site.com/p5

test_cases.input:
http://one.io/
http://one.io/ http://one.io/a
http://one.io/a http://two.io/x
http://one.io/a http://one.io/b

http://c.co/1
http://c.co/1 http://c.co/2
http://c.co/2 http://c.co/3
http://c.co/3 http://c.co/4
http://c.co/4 http://c.co/5
http://c.co/5 http://c.co/6
http://c.co/6 http://c.co/7
http://c.co/7 http://c.co/8
test_cases.expected_output:
http://c.co/1
http://c.co/2
http://c.co/3
http://c.co/4
http://c.co/5
http://c.co/6
http://c.co/7
http://c.co/8
